resources.string: Sesame stores your building access codes and automatically notifies you when you arrive at a saved location — so you never have to dig through your phone to find a code at the door.
All sensitive data is encrypted on your device using hardware-backed AES-256-GCM. Your access codes are never readable by anyone else.
Your access codes, always at hand.
Enter an address to look up.
No results found for this address.
Tap "Look up address" to convert it to GPS coordinates.
This entry has no resolved location. Geofencing won\'t work until you edit it and look up a valid address.
The PIN or access code for this location.
Tap + to add your first location and access code.
A name to identify this location.
Directions once inside. This will appear in notifications.
e.g. 3rd floor, left corridor, green door
Add any address with its access code and optional notes.
Sesame detects when you arrive and shows you the code automatically.
All codes are encrypted on your device using hardware-backed security. Only you can read them.

When granting location access, make sure to keep "Use precise location" enabled. You\'ll then be taken to a second screen to set it to "Allow all the time" — both are required for automatic arrival alerts.

Sesame needs precise location access and notifications to alert you when you arrive at a saved address. Without these, the app can still store your codes safely — but won\'t be able to notify you automatically.
Start by adding your first location. Sesame will take care of the rest.
Go to Settings → Security and enable a strong screen lock (PIN, pattern, or fingerprint)
Set up fingerprint or face unlock for quick, secure access
Your codes are hardware-encrypted and cannot be read without your device\'s unique security key
Sesame Lite encrypts your codes with your device\'s hardware security chip. Your screen lock is the primary protection for your data.
Your building access codes, always at hand — and delivered to you automatically when you need them.
Location permission is required for arrival alerts to work.
Precise location is required. Open Settings and enable "Use precise location" for Sesame.
Background location access is required for arrival alerts when the app is closed.
Location services are off. Enable them in Settings to receive arrival alerts.
Notifications are disabled. Sesame won\'t be able to alert you when you arrive at a saved location.
Could not generate QR code. Make sure the entry is fully saved.
Label, address and access code are always included.
Share via Messages, email or more…
The shared link can be opened in Sesame, Sesame Lite, or in any web browser.
This QR code contains all the shared fields in plain text. Only share it with trusted people.
Recipients without Sesame installed will not be able to open this link.
How close you need to be to the location before receiving the notification. %1$s is recommended for most buildings.
This will show the key unavailable banner as if iCloud Keychain had failed. The app will re-check periodically.
Notifications when you arrive at a saved location
This entry was created with a newer version of Sesame. Please update the app to import it.
This link doesn\'t appear to be a valid Sesame share link.
Your encrypted entries can\'t be decrypted because the encryption key is missing. This can happen if the app was reinstalled. You can reset all data to start fresh.
This permanently deletes all entries and removes the encryption key. There is no undo.
Check app settings to manage storage and permissions.
This will permanently delete all entries and the encryption key. This cannot be undone.
Encryption key unavailable — some entries can\'t be decrypted.
The encryption key is not available. Your entries cannot be saved at this time.
The clipboard contains a Sesame link. Importing it will replace the current form contents.
The clipboard doesn\'t contain a valid Sesame link.
This entry will be permanently deleted.
An entry with the same label and address already exists. You can replace it with this one, save as a separate entry, or go back to keep editing.
